l1: Has the domain sustained a total forest outage or single server failure?
Are valid System State backups or full bare-metal images currently available?
Which Domain Controller holds the critical FSMO roles inside the domain structure?
Is the outage impacting authentication, DNS resolution, or Group Policy processing?
Are end users unable to log in or access shared resources due to the failure?
l2: Is an authoritative or non-authoritative restore required for SYSVOL components?
What is the current Tombstone Lifetime configuration setting for the forest objects?
Are isolated recovery environments networks prepared without any production links?
Is the forest functional level compatible with the recovery method being attempted?
Are backup Domain Controllers available in other sites for role transfer or rebuild?

l1: Run 'wbadmin get versions' to list available local system recovery points.
Run 'netdom query fsmo' to see current infrastructure master assignments.
Check event logs for directory service corruption errors such as Event 1003.
Run 'dcdiag /test:DNS' to confirm DNS service health on surviving DCs.
Check if SYSVOL and Netlogon shares are accessible from client machines.
l2: Run 'ntdsutil "activate instance ntds" semantic db analysis info' for health evaluation.
Verify status of NTDS files utilizing 'esentutl /g <path_to_ntds.dit>'.
Verify backup capabilities by validating VSS writers output strings state data.
Run 'repadmin /showrepl' to identify replication failures across sites.
Check Kerberos authentication latency using 'klist' and Event ID 4768 logs.

l1: Boot into Directory Services Repair Mode (DSRM) utilizing local recovery passwords.
Restore system state images utilizing wbadmin administration command interface.
Seize outstanding FSMO roles using ntdsutil if original holders are dead.
Rebuild DNS zones from backup if primary DNS service is corrupted.
Re-enable SYSVOL replication using 'ntfrsutl' if replication is halted.
l2: Perform metadata cleanup using ntdsutil for removed offline domain controller nodes.
Execute authoritative restore configurations on target OUs utilizing ntdsutil utility.
Fix invalid USN states using strict isolation parameters during server cold boots.
Reconfigure replication topology using 'repadmin /replsummary' and site link adjustments.
Restore schema master role from backup if schema corruption is detected.

l1: Capture screens and outputs showing wbadmin system state histories records.
Export the setup and directory service configuration event logs to disks.
Log the network topologies maps and active IP allocation configurations.
Collect 'ipconfig /all' outputs from affected servers and clients.
Document FSMO role holders before and after recovery attempts.
l2: Gather esentutl database integrity outputs and diagnostic parsing text reports.
Export systemic registry hives detailing storage configurations parameters settings.
Collect Active Directory schema configuration dumps matching forest structures.
Export replication logs and Event Viewer Directory Service logs from all DCs.
Capture Wireshark traces during failed authentication attempts.

metadata_cleanup.title: Performing Metadata Cleanup for a Failed Domain Controller

steps.expect: Confirm target dead domain controller is completely unreachable and unrecoverable
Retrieve object properties to verify its current NTDS settings path exists in configuration partition
Deletes computer account; modern OS automatically triggers metadata cleanup for the associated DC objects

steps.action: ntdsutil "metadata cleanup" "connections" "connect to server local_dc" "quit" "select operation target" "list servers in site"
steps.expect: Verify the failed domain controller no longer shows up in the server inventory list
